entries.statement: Knowledge of SQL for data querying and manipulation, including joins, aggregations, window functions, subqueries, and query optimization fundamentals.

Knowledge of Python programming language for data applications, including data structures, control flow, object-oriented and functional programming patterns, standard libraries, and the data science ecosystem (NumPy, Pandas, scikit-learn).

Knowledge of relational database management system principles and practices, including normalization theory, indexing strategies, transaction isolation levels, query execution plans, and performance optimization.

Knowledge of NoSQL database categories and their use cases, including document stores, key-value stores, wide-column stores, graph databases, and vector databases, with understanding of when each is preferred over relational alternatives.

Knowledge of cloud platform services for data and AI workloads across major providers, including compute, storage, managed database, analytics, and machine learning services, with understanding of cloud-native vs cloud-agnostic trade-offs.

Knowledge of distributed computing concepts relevant to data processing at scale, including data partitioning strategies, parallel execution models, fault tolerance mechanisms, data locality optimization, and the CAP theorem.

Knowledge of data serialization formats and their trade-offs for different workloads, including JSON, Apache Avro, Apache Parquet, and Protocol Buffers, with understanding of schema evolution, compression characteristics, and query performance implications.

Knowledge of software version control systems and Git workflows, including branching strategies, merge and rebase operations, conflict resolution, pull request processes, and collaborative development practices.

Knowledge of continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipeline concepts and practices, including continuous integration principles, automated testing integration, artifact management, deployment automation patterns, and environment promotion strategies.

Knowledge of containerization and container orchestration concepts for data workloads, including Docker container principles, image layering and optimization, Kubernetes pod and service architecture, and resource management for data-intensive containers.

Knowledge of API design patterns and communication protocols for data services, including REST architectural constraints, GraphQL query language and schema design, gRPC service definitions and protocol buffers, WebSocket real-time communication, message queue protocols for asynchronous integration, authentication and authorization mechanisms, and API versioning strategies.

Knowledge of Linux operating system fundamentals relevant to data engineering, including file system structure, process management, user and permission models, package management, and common command-line utilities for text processing and system monitoring.

Knowledge of networking fundamentals relevant to data systems, including TCP/IP protocol suite, DNS resolution, HTTP/HTTPS protocols, firewall and security group configurations, load balancing, and how network architecture affects data pipeline design and performance.

Knowledge of computing fundamentals relevant to data and AI work, including computer architecture (processor architectures and instruction sets, memory hierarchy and cache behavior, storage systems and device characteristics), operating system principles, hardware-software interaction, and how infrastructure constraints affect data processing performance and system design decisions.

Knowledge of modern software development methodologies and practices, including Agile frameworks, Waterfall and iterative models, Test-Driven Development, DevOps and DevSecOps principles, and their application to data and AI projects.

Knowledge of Infrastructure-as-Code, cloud-native development patterns, and platform engineering practices, including containerized infrastructure design, configuration management, service mesh concepts, and orchestration of multi-container applications.

Knowledge of DevOps and MLOps operational practices for data and AI systems, including system monitoring and observability, logging and tracing, performance metrics collection, incident detection, and automated remediation workflows.

Knowledge of operating system fundamentals and kernel concepts relevant to data engineering, including virtualization mechanisms, memory management and paging, process scheduling, I/O systems, file system operations, and resource allocation.

Knowledge of system performance optimization and tuning principles for data systems, including profiling and bottleneck analysis, resource utilization optimization, query optimization, caching strategies, and trade-offs between performance and resource consumption.

Knowledge of data partitioning, sharding, and replication strategies in distributed systems, including partition key selection, replication models, consistency levels, and trade-offs between availability, partition tolerance, and consistency.

Knowledge of automated testing and quality assurance practices for software and data systems, including unit testing frameworks, integration testing, performance and load testing, chaos engineering, test automation in CI/CD pipelines, and test metrics.

Knowledge of data workflow orchestration and pipeline execution frameworks, including directed acyclic graph (DAG) execution models, task scheduling and dependency management, error handling and retry mechanisms, and monitoring of complex data pipelines.
